mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in >65% of months where FEDFUNDS increases, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer spending, which reduces corporate profit expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >60% of triggered instances, or the signal loses predictive power when UNRATE remains below 4.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP growth and moderate CPI inflation indicates robust aggregate demand without triggering aggressive monetary tightening, supporting higher corporate revenues and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative returns in >55% of quarters, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in out-of-sample walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in the index of industrial production reflect expanding manufacturing capacity and supply-side momentum, which translates into immediate positive market sentiment and positive S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in >60% of months with positive INDPRO growth, or autocorrelation in returns fully explains the signal.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms broad-based labor market strength, boosting consumer confidence and corporate earnings forecasts to drive positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to predict positive returns when GDP growth falls below 1.5 percent, or Rank IC drops below 0.01 over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in retail sales exceeding 1.0 percent signals a sharp contraction in consumer demand, forcing firms to revise earnings downward and triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in >65% of triggered months, or the threshold effect vanishes when controlling for seasonal adjustments.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation runs above 3.0 percent annually while the federal funds rate remains elevated, equity valuations face dual pressure from eroding real returns and higher discount rates, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Returns are positive in >60% of periods meeting the inflation and rate criteria, or the mechanism shows no predictive edge outside of high-inflation regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Increases in housing starts reflect rising residential investment and construction activity, which stimulates upstream supply chains and signals economic resilience, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in >65% of months with positive HOUST growth, or the signal is fully explained by concurrent interest rate movements.
